entry.title: Hello World
entry.content: Welcome to Yum1era’s Blog!

entry.title: Function
entry.content:
An inverse function , denoted as f−1f{-1}f−1 ,is a function that “reverses” another function fff .If fff maps xxx to yyy (i.e.,y=f(x)y=f(x)y=f(x)),then its inverse function f−1f^{-1}f−1 maps yyy back to xxx(i.e.,x=f−1(y)x=f^{-1}(y)x=f−1(y)).For a function to have an inverse,it must be bijective(both injective and surjective),meaning each element of the domain maps to exactly one element in the codomain,and every element in the codomain is mapped to by exactly one element form the domain.

Here are some key points about inverse functions.

1.Domain and Range Swap:The domain of the original function becomes the range of its inverse,and the range of the original function becomes the domain of its inverse.

2.Graphical Symmetry:The graphs of a function and its inverse are symmetrical with respect to the line y=x.

3.Finding Inverse Functions:To find the inverse of a function,you typically set y=f(x)y=f(x)y=f(x) ,solve for xxx in terms of yyy ,and then express the result as f−1(x)f^{-1}(x)f−1(x) .

4.Existence Conditions:A function has an inverse if and only if it is monotonic over its domain(either strictly increasing or strictly decresing)

For example,consider the function f(x)=2x+3f(x)=2x+3f(x)=2x+3.To find its inverse:

Start with y=2x+3y=2x+3y=2x+3

Solve for x:y−3=2xx:y-3=2xx:y−3=2x

Therefor,x=y−32x=\frac{y-3}{2}x=2y−3​

So,the inverse function is f−1(x)=x−32f^{-1}(x)=\frac{x-3}{2}f−1(x)=2x−3​ .

Definition of the Limit of a Sequence

Let {ana{_n}an​} be a sequence of real numbers.We say that the sequence {ana_nan​} converges to a limit LLL(or has the limit LLL ) if for every ε>0ε>0ε>0,there exists a positive integer NNN such that for all n>Nn>Nn>N ,the inequality ∣an−L∣<ε|a_n-L|<ε∣an​−L∣<ε holds.This is written as:

lim⁡a→+∞an=L\displaystyle\lim_{a \to +\infty}a_n= La→+∞lim​an​=L

In other words,for any given ε>0ε>0ε>0 ,no matter how small,we can find a positive integer NNN such that the terms of the sequence {ana_nan​} are within εεε of LLL for all n>Nn>Nn>N

Example

To illustrate this definition,consider the sequence an=1na_n=\frac{1}{n}an​=n1​

1:Given ∀ε>0{\forall}ε>0∀ε>0 ，∣Xn−a∣<ε|X_n-a|<ε∣Xn​−a∣<ε

2.Find NNN such that for all n>Nn>Nn>N ,∣an−0<ε∣|a_n-0<ε|∣an​−0<ε∣.

3.Simplify the inequality. ∣1n−0∣=1n|\frac{1}{n}-0|=\frac{1}{n}∣n1​−0∣=n1​

We need 1n<ε\frac{1}{n}<εn1​<ε.

4:Solve the inequality.

n>1εn>\frac{1}{ε}n>ε1​

Choose NNN to be the smallest integer.i.e.,N=[1ε]N=[\frac{1}{ε}]N=[ε1​]

Step 3:Verify.For n>Nn>Nn>N ,we have n>1εn>\frac{1}{ε}n>ε1​ ,so 1n<ε\frac{1}{n}<εn1​<ε ,which means ∣1n−0∣<ε|\frac{1}{n}-0|<ε∣n1​−0∣<ε.

Thus,we have shown that lim⁡n→+∞1n=0\displaystyle\lim_{n \to +\infty}\frac{1}{n}=0n→+∞lim​n1​=0

entry.title: Mapping
entry.content:
Mapping:A mapping, or map, is a rule that assigns to each element of one set, called the domain, an element of another set, called the codomain.

Partial Mapping:A partial mapping is a mapping where some elements of the domain may not be assigned to any element in the codomain.

Total Mapping:A total mapping is a mapping where every element of the domain is assigned to exactly one element in the codemain.

Inverse Mapping:If there is a mapping fff from setA to setB,then its inverse mapping
f−1f^{-1}f−1 is a mapping from setB back to setA.The inverse mapping exists if and only if the original mapping fff is bijective,meaning it is both injective(one-to-one) and surjective(onto),For a bijective mapping fff ,its inverse f−1f^{-1}f−1 is also bijective,and for every element yyy in B,there exists a unique element xxx in A such that f(x)=yf(x)=yf(x)=y and f−1(y)=xf^{-1}(y)=xf−1(y)=x
